public String findFilesWithPattern(String searchDir, String filePattern) {
    // Validate input parameters
    if (searchDir == null || searchDir.trim().isEmpty()) {
        return "Error: Search directory cannot be null or empty.";
    }
    
    if (filePattern == null || filePattern.trim().isEmpty()) {
        return "Error: File pattern cannot be null or empty.";
    }
    
    // Clean up inputs
    String cleanedDir = searchDir.trim();
    String cleanedPattern = filePattern.trim();
    
    // Validate that search directory exists and is a directory
    File dirFile = new File(cleanedDir);
    if (!dirFile.exists() || !dirFile.isDirectory()) {
        return "Error: Invalid search directory: " + cleanedDir;
    }
    
    // Build the find command with proper escaping
    String findCommand = String.format(
        "find \"%s\" -type f -name \"%s\" -print | head -n 10",
        escapeBashString(cleanedDir),
        escapeBashString(cleanedPattern)
    );
    
    try {
        // Use bash -c for proper shell execution
        Process process = Runtime.getRuntime().exec(new String[]{"bash", "-c", findCommand});
        
        // Wait for the process to complete
        int exitCode = process.waitFor();
        
        // Read the output
        List<String> outputLines = readProcessOutput(process);
        
        // Check exit code and handle errors
        if (exitCode != 0) {
            List<String> errorLines = readProcessError(process);
            return "Error: Command execution failed with exit code " + exitCode + 
                   (errorLines.isEmpty() ? "" : ": " + String.join(", ", errorLines));
        }
        
        // Return results (limited to 10 by the find command)
        return String.join("\n", outputLines);
        
    } catch (IOException e) {
        return "Error: IOException occurred while executing command: " + e.getMessage();
    } catch (InterruptedException e) {
        Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
        return "Error: Command execution was interrupted: " + e.getMessage();
    } catch (Exception e) {
        return "Error: Unexpected error occurred: " + e.getMessage();
    }
}

private String escapeBashString(String input) {
    if (input == null) return "";
    
    // Escape all special characters that could be dangerous in bash
    return input
        .replace("\\", "\\\\")
        .replace("\"", "\\\"")
        .replace("'", "'\\''")
        .replace("$", "\\$")
        .replace("`", "\\`")
        .replace(";", "\\;")
        .replace("|", "\\|")
        .replace("&", "\\&")
        .replace("<", "\\<")
        .replace(">", "\\>")
        .replace("(", "\\(")
        .replace(")", "\\)")
        .replace("{", "\\{")
        .replace("}", "\\}")
        .replace("[", "\\[")
        .replace("]", "\\]")
        .replace("*", "\\*")
        .replace("?", "\\?");
}

private List<String> readProcessOutput(Process process) throws IOException {
    List<String> lines = new ArrayList<>();
    try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(
            new InputStreamReader(process.getInputStream()))) {
        String line;
        while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null) {
            lines.add(line);
        }
    }
    return lines;
}

private List<String> readProcessError(Process process) throws IOException {
    List<String> lines = new ArrayList<>();
    try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(
            new InputStreamReader(process.getErrorStream()))) {
        String line;
        while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null) {
            lines.add(line);
        }
    }
    return lines;
}

I'll methodically reconstruct the solution with robust input sanitization and defensive coding practices.

package com.example.service;

import java.io.BufferedReader;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.io.InputStream;
import java.io.InputStreamReader;
import java.util.regex.Pattern;

/**
 * File service class providing functionality to find files by pattern
 */
public class FileService {

    // Pattern to validate file pattern - allows alphanumeric, *, ?, ., -, _, /, and brackets
    private static final Pattern SAFE_FILE_PATTERN = Pattern.compile("^[a-zA-Z0-9*?\\.\\-_/\\[\\]]+$");

    /**
     * Find files matching the specified pattern
     *
     * @param searchDir   Path of the directory to search
     * @param filePattern Filename pattern (supports wildcards)
     * @return List of found files, one per line; returns error message on failure
     */
    public String findFilesWithPattern(String searchDir, String filePattern) {
        // 0. Validate input parameters
        if (searchDir == null || filePattern == null || searchDir.trim().isEmpty()) {
            return "Error: Search directory or file pattern is empty.";
        }

        // 0a. Validate searchDir - must be an absolute path starting with /
        String trimmedDir = searchDir.trim();
        if (!trimmedDir.startsWith("/")) {
            return "Error: Search directory must be an absolute path starting with '/'.";
        }
        
        // Normalize and validate searchDir path
        String normalizedDir = normalizePath(trimmedDir);
        if (normalizedDir == null) {
            return "Error: Invalid directory path.";
        }

        // 0b. Validate filePattern for shell injection characters
        if (!isValidFilePattern(filePattern)) {
            return "Error: File pattern contains invalid characters. Only alphanumeric, *, ?, ., -, _, /, and [] are allowed.";
        }

        // 1. Construct shell command with properly sanitized arguments
        // Use single quotes around the pattern to prevent shell interpretation
        // Additionally escape any single quotes within the pattern itself
        String escapedPattern = escapeSingleQuotes(filePattern);
        String escapedDir = escapeShellArg(normalizedDir);
        String cmdStr = String.format("find \"%s\" -type f -name '%s' -print | head -n 10", 
                                      escapedDir, escapedPattern);
        String[] command = {"/bin/bash", "-c", cmdStr};

        Process process = null;
        try {
            // 2. Execute command using ProcessBuilder for better security
            ProcessBuilder pb = new ProcessBuilder(command);
            // Remove dangerous environment variables to prevent exploits
            pb.environment().remove("LD_PRELOAD");
            pb.environment().remove("LD_LIBRARY_PATH");
            
            process = pb.start();

            // 3. Read output and error streams
            // Important: Must read both streams to avoid blocking
            StringBuilder output = new StringBuilder();
            StringBuilder error = new StringBuilder();
            
            // Read output stream
            try (BufferedReader outputReader = new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(process.getInputStream()))) {
                String line;
                while ((line = outputReader.readLine()) != null) {
                    output.append(line).append("\n");
                }
            }
            
            // Read error stream
            try (BufferedReader errorReader = new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(process.getErrorStream()))) {
                String line;
                while ((line = errorReader.readLine()) != null) {
                    error.append(line).append("\n");
                }
            }

            // 4. Wait for command execution to complete
            int exitCode = process.waitFor();

            // 5. Determine result based on exit code and output
            if (exitCode != 0) {
                // Command execution failed, return error information
                return "Error: File search failed.\n" + error.toString();
            }

            String outputStr = output.toString();
            if (outputStr.isEmpty()) {
                return "No matching files found.";
            }

            return outputStr.trim();
        } catch (IOException | InterruptedException e) {
            if (e instanceof InterruptedException) {
                Thread.currentThread().interrupt(); // Re-set interrupt status
            }
            return "Error: Exception occurred while executing command: " + e.getMessage();
        } finally {
            if (process != null) {
                // Ensure child process is destroyed
                process.destroy();
            }
        }
    }

    /**
     * Validates the file pattern to ensure it only contains safe characters
     * 
     * @param pattern The file pattern to validate
     * @return true if pattern is safe, false otherwise
     */
    private boolean isValidFilePattern(String pattern) {
        if (pattern == null || pattern.isEmpty()) {
            return false;
        }
        
        // Check against whitelist pattern
        return SAFE_FILE_PATTERN.matcher(pattern).matches();
    }

    /**
     * Escapes a string for safe use as a shell argument enclosed in double quotes
     * 
     * @param arg The argument to escape
     * @return The escaped argument
     */
    private String escapeShellArg(String arg) {
        if (arg == null) {
            return "";
        }
        // Escape double quotes, backslashes, and $ signs for double-quoted shell args
        return arg.replace("\\", "\\\\")
                  .replace("\"", "\\\"")
                  .replace("$", "\\$");
    }

    /**
     * Escapes single quotes within the pattern for single-quoted shell arguments
     * 
     * In bash, to include a single quote inside a single-quoted string,
     * you must: end the single quote, add an escaped single quote, start single quotes again
     * 
     * @param pattern The pattern to escape
     * @return The escaped pattern
     */
    private String escapeSingleQuotes(String pattern) {
        if (pattern == null) {
            return "";
        }
        // Replace single quote with: single quote, backslash single quote, single quote
        // This properly escapes single quotes within single-quoted strings
        return pattern.replace("'", "'\\''");
    }

    /**
     * Normalizes and validates the directory path to prevent path traversal attacks
     * 
     * @param path The path to normalize
     * @return The normalized path, or null if invalid
     */
    private String normalizePath(String path) {
        if (path == null || path.isEmpty()) {
            return null;
        }
        
        // Remove any null bytes
        String cleaned = path.replace("\0", "");
        
        // Ensure the path is absolute and doesn't contain wildcards
        if (!cleaned.startsWith("/") || cleaned.contains("*") || cleaned.contains("?")) {
            return null;
        }
        
        return cleaned;
    }
}

package com.example.service;

import java.io.BufferedReader;
import java.io.InputStreamReader;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.List;
import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;
import java.util.regex.Pattern;

/**
 * Service class for file search operations using find command
 */
public class FileService {

    // Pattern to validate safe file/directory names (alphanumeric, underscore, hyphen, dot, slash)
    private static final Pattern SAFE_PATH_PATTERN = Pattern.compile("^[a-zA-Z0-9_./-]+$");
    
    // Pattern for file name (alphanumeric, underscore, hyphen, dot, asterisk, question mark)
    private static final Pattern SAFE_PATTERN_PATTERN = Pattern.compile("^[a-zA-Z0-9_.*?-]+$");

    /**
     * Searches for files matching the specified pattern in the given directory
     * 
     * @param searchDir Directory path to search in
     * @param filePattern File pattern with wildcard support (e.g., *.txt, *.java)
     * @return List of found files separated by newlines, or error message if search fails
     */
    public String findFilesWithPattern(String searchDir, String filePattern) {
        // Validate input parameters
        if (searchDir == null || searchDir.trim().isEmpty()) {
            return "Error: Search directory cannot be empty";
        }
        if (filePattern == null || filePattern.trim().isEmpty()) {
            return "Error: File pattern cannot be empty";
        }

        // Normalize and validate paths
        String normalizedDir = searchDir.trim();
        String normalizedPattern = filePattern.trim();

        // Validate directory path for security
        if (!isValidPath(normalizedDir)) {
            return "Error: Invalid characters in search directory path";
        }

        // Validate pattern for security
        if (!isValidPattern(normalizedPattern)) {
            return "Error: Invalid characters in file pattern";
        }

        Process process = null;
        StringBuilder output = new StringBuilder();
        StringBuilder errorOutput = new StringBuilder();

        try {
            // Use ProcessBuilder with proper argument handling - no shell injection risk
            List<String> command = new ArrayList<>();
            command.add("find");
            command.add(normalizedDir);
            command.add("-type");
            command.add("f");
            command.add("-name");
            command.add(normalizedPattern);
            command.add("-print");
            command.add("-maxdepth");  // Add depth limit for security
            command.add("5");

            ProcessBuilder processBuilder = new ProcessBuilder(command);
            processBuilder.redirectErrorStream(false); // Keep stdout and stderr separate

            process = processBuilder.start();

            // Read stdout and stderr concurrently to prevent blocking
            Thread outputThread = new Thread(() -> {
                try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(
                        new InputStreamReader(process.getInputStream()))) {
                    String line;
                    boolean firstLine = true;
                    while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null) {
                        if (!firstLine) {
                            output.append("\n");
                        }
                        output.append(line);
                        firstLine = false;
                        // Limit output size to prevent memory issues
                        if (output.length() > 100000) {
                            output.append("\n... output truncated");
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                } catch (IOException e) {
                    // Ignore
                }
            });

            Thread errorThread = new Thread(() -> {
                try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(
                        new InputStreamReader(process.getErrorStream()))) {
                    String line;
                    while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null) {
                        if (errorOutput.length() > 0) {
                            errorOutput.append("\n");
                        }
                        errorOutput.append(line);
                    }
                } catch (IOException e) {
                    // Ignore
                }
            });

            outputThread.start();
            errorThread.start();

            // Wait for process completion with timeout
            boolean finished = process.waitFor(30, TimeUnit.SECONDS);
            
            if (!finished) {
                process.destroyForcibly();
                return "Error: Command execution timed out";
            }

            outputThread.join(1000);
            errorThread.join(1000);

            int exitCode = process.exitValue();

            if (exitCode != 0) {
                String errors = errorOutput.toString();
                if (errors.isEmpty()) {
                    errors = "Command execution failed with exit code: " + exitCode;
                }
                return "Error: " + errors;
            }

            // Return search results
            String result = output.toString();
            if (result.isEmpty()) {
                return "No files found matching pattern";
            }
            return result;

        } catch (IOException e) {
            return "Error: I/O exception occurred - " + e.getMessage();
        } catch (InterruptedException e) {
            Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
            return "Error: Process was interrupted - " + e.getMessage();
        } catch (Exception e) {
            return "Error: Unexpected error occurred - " + e.getMessage();
        } finally {
            // Ensure process is destroyed
            if (process != null && process.isAlive()) {
                process.destroyForcibly();
            }
        }
    }

    /**
     * Validates that a path contains only safe characters
     * 
     * @param path Path to validate
     * @return true if path is valid, false otherwise
     */
    private boolean isValidPath(String path) {
        // Remove trailing slashes for validation
        String cleanPath = path.replaceAll("/+$", "");
        return SAFE_PATH_PATTERN.matcher(cleanPath).matches();
    }

    /**
     * Validates that a file pattern contains only safe characters
     * 
     * @param pattern Pattern to validate
     * @return true if pattern is valid, false otherwise
     */
    private boolean isValidPattern(String pattern) {
        return SAFE_PATTERN_PATTERN.matcher(pattern).matches();
    }
}
